ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
ache: verb. To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
achene: noun. A small, dry, indehiscent fruit, containing a single seed, as in the buttercup.
acne: noun. A skin condition, usually of the face, that is common in adolescents.
age: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Angal.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
axe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ayerrerenge.
cage: noun. An enclosure made of spaced bars, normally to hold animals while providing some ventilation.
chang: noun. A traditional harp of central and southwest Asia
each: determiner. All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
exchange: noun. An act of exchanging or trading.
exec: noun. An executive; a person employed at the executive level.
gae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Warekena.
gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
ghee: noun. A type of clarified butter used in South Asian cooking.
hae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Harar Oromo.
hag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hanga.
hang: verb. To be or remain suspended.
hen: noun. A female chicken (Gallus gallus), especially a sexually mature one kept for her eggs.
hence: adverb. From here, from this place, away.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
nag: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naga Pidgin.
nah: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Nahuan languages.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
